events.title: Author Event with Christopher Mirabile, The Washashore: A Seaside Mystery Thriller – Thursday, May 14th at 7pm
events.description: @ Please join us for a night of discussion and Q&A with Christopher Mirabile, author of The Washashore: A Seaside Mystery Thriller. The event will be held at The Book Shop of Needham on Thursday, May 14th at 7pm. “A thoroughly entertaining and suspenseful debut, from a writer to watch. The Washashore has action, terrific dialogue, and a charming cast of characters that will keep you hooked on this promising new series.” —William Landay, New York Times bestselling author of Defending Jacob A fatal late-night hit-and-run shatters the quiet of Provincetown, drawing interim police chief Silas Lopez into a case that appears straightforward—until the cracks begin to show. In a town where secrets wash ashore with every tide, nothing is ever as it seems. From windswept dunes and secretive cranberry farms to the gritty docks of New Bedford, Silas chases a trail of shifting lies—missing trucks, grainy night-vision photos, and a maze of half-truths—each step pulling him deeper into a game no one wants him to play. The deeper he digs, the more formidable the forces that rise to stop him. But Silas isn’t one to back down. A fifteenth-generation ranch hand turned plain-spoken cop, he’s fueled by grit, instinct, and a stubborn streak that won’t quit. With a young department still finding its footing and a growing bond with fiery social worker Wren Bradford, Silas presses on as the tide of danger rises around them. What begins as a tragic accident investigation unravels into something far darker and more dangerous, forcing Silas to confront a threat that could cost him everything he holds dear. Perfect for fans of Michael Connelly, Archer Mayor, and William Kent Krueger, this first Silas Lopez Mystery blends small-town intrigue, windswept coast beauty, criminal noir, and relentless suspense into a gripping debut that will keep fans of mystery series turning pages long into the night. events.title: Concert, Concert: “Voices and Songs of Liberty—A Patriotic Concert Celebrating the 250th Anniversary of the Birth of America”
events.description: Concert, “Voices and Songs of Liberty—A Patriotic Concert Celebrating the 250th Anniversary of the Birth of America” SPONSOR: Celebrations Committee as part of Wellesley Wonderful Weekend DATE/TIME: Thursday, May 14, 7:30pm-9pm LOCATION: Wellesley Hills Congregational Church, 207 Washington Street Buy tickets, click here DETAILS: Concert by Wellesley Symphony Orchestra; Wellesley Town Band; Wellesley Choral Society; and special guest soloist, Ayla Brown Concert Program PART I: BOSTON AND SURROUNDING TOWNS: A NATION FOR LIBERTY Wellesley Symphony Orchestra I. Overture: Boston, A City of Argument II. Benjamin Franklin: The Mind III. Samuel Adams: The Spark IV. Abigail Adams: The Conscience V. Collision: Ideas Become Action VI. Toward Independence VII. Coda: What Boston Gave the World PART II: A NATION INHERITED Wellesley Town Band, Wellesley Choral Society America the Beautiful Concert note: lyrics by Katharine Lee Bates—Wellesley’s own Katharine Lee Bates (1859-1929) graduated from Wellesley High School; wrote “America the Beautiful,”; worshiped at Wellesley Hills Congregational Church; and taught at Dana Hall School, and Wellesley College. Shenandoah This Land is Your Land Simple Gifts My Country ‘Tis of Thee PART III: special appearance by soloist Ayla Brown Our National Anthem will be performed by Ayla Brown, an American Idol TV show finalist who first sang in Wellesley as a teenager at Wellesley’s Annual Town Picnic stage two decades ago. Since then, she has appeared at some of the nation’s most iconic venues and events, including Monday Night Football, the Orange Bowl, presidential debates, the Boston Pops Orchestra, and for all of Boston’s professional sports teams. events.title: Yard Sale Fundraiser for 2026 Jimmy Fund Walk (May 16th and 17th, 9am-4pm)
events.description: @ HUGE ANNUAL YARD SALE: Local resident Alan Rubin is again holding a 2-day yard sale at 27 Cynthia Road in Needham to support the 2026 Jimmy Fund Walk fundraising efforts with 100% of the proceeds going to Dana-Farber in support of the fight against cancer. The Jimmy Fund Walk continues to raise the most money of any single-day walk in the nation. Last year’s Walk raised more than $11.2 million, with over 9300 walkers and 1000 volunteers. Once again, we will have a wide variety and large number of priced to sell items generously donated by friends and family. Many items will be marked with a “?”, or simply unmarked, allowing the buyer to determine what they wish to donate for that item, again in the spirit of this being a fundraiser. There will be something for everyone of all ages and interests, including complimentary Jimmy Fund hats while supplies last. You’ll find furniture, framed mirrors, novelty items, picture frames, framed prints and display artwork, games/toys/puzzles, high chair, Evenflow Exersaucer, large dollhouse, adult bikes, small kitchen appliances, electronics, serving pieces, dishware, vintage Noritaki china (7 piece settings for 12), glassware, silverware, housewares, antiques, table lamps, luggage, tote bags, sports cards/memorabilia, commemorative plate block stamp collection, throws/blankets, daybed comforter set, several new/like-new handbags and purses (many designer), yard and power tools, area rugs, cameras, watches, picnic baskets with accessories, Mackenzie-Childs dinner candles and holders, Lucid Candle brand liquid candles, scented wax melts and drizzles and soaps, and so much more as donated items continue to be accepted for this great cause! Rain dates if necessary, TBD. The Jimmy Fund Walk takes place on Sunday October 4, starting at 1 of 4 distance locations, that includes the Boston Marathon Course, and all finishing in celebration at the Boston Common. You can register to participate or donate at this link: http://danafarber.jimmyfund.org/goto/AlanRubin Add to calendar Google Calendar iCalendar Outlook 365 Outlook Live Details Date: Time:

events.title: Touch-A-Truck event at Wellesley DPW
events.description: Visit the Wellesley Department of Public Works (30 Municipal Way) on Saturday, May 16 from 9:00 a.m. to noon as part of Wellesley Wonderful Weekend. A sensory hour is planned from 11:00 a.m. to noon. During the sensory hour there will be no lights, no music, and no horn honking. A variety of equipment will be open for visitors to explore during the Touch-A-Truck event at DPW Headquarters (30 Municipal Way) including electric equipment, dump trucks, street sweepers, front loaders and more. This is a rain or shine event. In case of severe weather, event will be moved into DPW garage bays. Add to donation efforts this year. Donate business clothing to Suits for Success being given to Big Brother Big Sister. Bring a non-perishable food donation to Fill-A-Truck. All donated food will go to the Wellesley Food Pantry. Desired donations include: • Baked, Refried, White Beans, especially Chickpeas • Canned Fruit-In Fruit Juice, especially Pineapple • Canned Tomato Sauce • Beef, Lentil, Bean Soup • Broths-Unsalted, All Types in 32 oz. Cartons • Dinty Moore Beef Stew • Peanut Butter (16 oz. Jars), Crunchy & Creamy • Raisins • Coffee Questions? Contact the Wellesley DPW at 781-235-7600 ext. 3379 or email dpw@wellesleyma.gov Please send tips, photos, ideas to theswellesleyreport@gmail.com

events.title: Needham Garden Club Annual Plant Sale
events.description: On Saturday, 5/16/ from 9:00 – 1:00, the Needham Garden Club will host its annual Plant Sale outside of the Needham Congregational Church (1154 Great Plain Avenue, Needham). The sale will feature native plants which are hearty for our region and many plants from members’ gardens, including sun and shade perennials, grasses, herbs and ferns. Parking for the event is in the rear of the building across from the Post Office. Proceeds from the sale help sponsor educational programs in horticulture and floral design at the Needham Library; Garden Therapy sessions at local assisted living and nursing homes; a college scholarship for a Needham Senior planning to study biology, horticulture, conservation or environmental science; as well as plantings and maintenance of community garden spaces around town. With the proceeds from last year’s sale, we were also able to support the Needham High School Environmental Club and the Needham Community Farm.

events.title: All Aboard, an accessible and adaptive cycling program on Cochituate Rail Trail
events.description: The Town of Natick presents All Aboard, an accessible and adaptive cycling program. Anyone that has a mobility limitation and wants to experience cycling is welcome and encouraged to participate. The Friends of Natick Trails supports the All Aboard program. To register as a rider, click the link below: May 16, 2026 Time slots: 10am-3pm Meeting spot: 11 Mechanic St., Natick, MA 01760 Natick is partnering with All Out Adventures, who brings a fleet of adaptive and accessible tricycles and bicycles to Natick on scheduled Saturdays. Staff will ensure a proper fit, and volunteers are available to accompany riders. All rides will start at the Mechanic Street lot on the Cochituate Rail Trail. This program is free to riders. Please note that advance registration is required for riders and volunteers.

events.title: Wellesley Trails Committee Guided Walk—Hemlock Gorge
events.description: Join the Wellesley Trails Committee on a guided trail walk. Free. No registration required. Walks take place unless it pours rain or there are high winds. Dogs on leashes are welcome. DATE: Saturday, May 16, 2026 TIME: 10am-11:15am LOCATION: Hemlock Gorge (1.0 miles). Meet at the Echo Bridge Office Park parking lot, at 381 Elliot Street, Newton. Park on the side of the lot facing the river. DESCRIPTION: Chances are you never visited Hemlock Gorge and walked its near silent trails. And chances are you never trod the deck of the seven-arched Echo Bridge and looked down upon what was once a field dotted with wigwams. Come explore the trails and bridge in Hemlock Gorge, a scenic and historic area along the Charles River at the corners of Wellesley, Needham and Newton. While there, you might hear the echo of your own voice and imagine the sounds of a bygone time.

events.title: Holi Festival in Natick Center
events.description: Annual Holi Festival in Natick Center (Middlesex Savings Bank Parking Lot/Rt. 135 & Washington St.) This free, annual community event organized by the Natick Center Cultural District features, music, dancing, and eco-friendly powdered colors. Holi (pronounced /holi/; Sanskrit: हो ली Holi) is a spring festival that is celebrated in the Indian subcontinent. It is popularly known as the “festival of colors” or “festival of love.” You can revel in the festivities with eco-friendly dry powdered colors, while grooving to the lively Bollywood music that our fabulous DJ has in store for you. Enjoy a Bollywood dance lesson while you attend. Rain Date, May 18 from 2- 4:00 pm.

events.title: Exclusive Showing: The Day Iceland Stood Still
events.description: Progressive Needham and the Center at the Heights are pleased to present an exclusive screening of the award-winning documentary, The Day Iceland Stood Still. The film is an iconic depiction of a successful social strike. The day in 1975 that 90% of women in Iceland ‘took the day off’ rocked the nation and began a movement that has propelled the country from the bottom to the top of world gender equality indexes. The documentary uses personal stories and animation to show how this massive, humorous, and urgent act of solidarity launched Iceland into a global leader of gender equality. Stay for a conversation with Fredie Kay from the Women’s History Center and Deb Paul from Neighborhood United Indivisible following the film.

events.title: Wellesley Conservation Land Trust’s Spring Lecture—”The Role of Forests in Decarbonization”
events.description: How have the forests of New England evolved since the post-colonial period and how might they save us in the future? These are complex questions involving the ways biological, physical, and human systems interact to shape the region, including all the ways the landscape is changing due to both global forces such as climate change and local forces like land use. Central to our future is the role that forests play in decarbonization and the land use tradeoffs that may lead to net zero. events.title: King’s Own Come to Needham!
events.description: The British Are Coming! On Saturday, May 30th Needham will once again host The King’s Own, a group of reenactors who portray the British Redcoats who sought to prevent the American Revolution. Adults, teens, and children are invited to attend the fun, educational event that helps commemorate the 250th Anniversary of the founding of our country. See authentic uniforms, weapons, and drill, ask questions, and learn about the challenging days that led ordinary people of Needham into direct confrontation with their King and his army. Learn how British soldiers lived, fought, and made their way though seven years of warfare from Canada to the Caribbean. This event is sponsored by the Needham Cultural Council and is free to all.

events.title: Hunnewell Estate Historical Tour
events.description: Saturday, May 30, 2026, 9:45 AM-12:00 PM The Tollhouse Shop, 229 Washington Street, Wellesley, MA, 02481 Space is limited! We have 24 spaces available per tour. Tickets here. Experience the rich history and vibrant present of the Hunnewell Estate with the Wellesley Historical Society’s exclusive walking tour. This 90-minute journey delves into the estate’s unique horticultural collection, offering insights into both its past and present. With only 24 spaces available per tour, this intimate experience ensures a detailed exploration of the gardens. Please note that the tour includes uneven terrain and requires all participants to use the provided transportation. Proceeds from the tour will benefit the Society’s general operating fund, supporting our mission to preserve local history.

events.title: Natick Garden Club Plant Sale
events.description: The Natick Garden Club is hosting its annual plant sale on Sunday, May 31 from 10am-2pm indoors at the Natick Community-Senior Center, 117 East Central St. The sale is the group’s major fundraiser for the year. They offer a variety of plants that have been proven to grow successfully in our sometimes challenging weather conditions. Most of the plants are perennials from members’ gardens, and are labeled for bloom time and season, height, spacing needed, and sun and moisture requirements. They promote native plants as well, meaning they are native to our area, and tolerant of drought conditions and excellent at providing habitat for good insects and birds. There will be a modest houseplant and seedling section. Raffle tickets for a variety of items will be available and you don’t need to be present to win. events.title: “Regenerative Gardening in Today’s World”
events.description: @ Trevor Smith, an award-winning regenerative landscape designer and a principal of CFS Regenerative Design Studios, will discuss practices that climate change has brought to home gardening: planting native species to preserve biodiversity, combatting invasive species, building healthy soils, installing green infrastructure and managing water. The program will follow a very brief Friends’ Annual Meeting on June 7, 2026 at 2:00 p.m. at the Needham Free Public Library. Mr. Smith is the founder of ReEarth Solutions, a Boston-area ecological landscape design practice. He is passionate about the natural world which inspires his commitment to sustainable landscaping practices in an era of climate change. He is also passionate about sharing his knowledge and experience and is the host of the podcast “Peace, Love & Pollinators;” the live, online, “ask the expert” event Tuesdays with Trevor; and the Hive-Mind for Business Owners, a program of the Ecological Landscape Alliance. The public is invited to this free program presented by the Friends of the Needham Public Library in the Library’s Community Room at 1139 Highland Avenue, Needham, from 2-3:30 PM, Sunday, June 7. A question-and answer period will follow the presentation. Seats are limited and registration is required. Register at https://needhamlibrary.org/events/. For more information, please contact Gay Ellen Dennett, program specialist, at gdennett@minlib.net, 781-455-7559 x223. DATE: Saturday, June 13 TIME: 10am-11:15am LOCATION: Meet at the Mass Bay parking lot off of Oakland Street, (not the Centennial lot) DESCRIPTION: Centennial Reservation (1.8 miles). Take a picturesque walk, through rolling meadows to the top of Maugus Hill, one of the highest points in Wellesley. View the beautiful Great Blue Hills in Canton from a hillside meadow overlooking the town of Needham. Then, descend through the meadow and around Benzanson Pond, a favorite swimming hole for dogs as well as frogs and tadpoles. Come explore this conservation land purchased by the town for Wellesley’s 1981 Centennial Celebration and now one of the most popular trails in Wellesley. This walk also includes Mass Bay land that is maintained by the Wellesley Trails Committee and the Wellesley DPW.

events.description: Rules of the Ride is a fun, hands-on way to learn bike handling and confidence for 5th to 8th graders. This year’s event is Sunday, June 7 from 10 a.m. to 11:30 a.m. at the Wellesley High School parking lot. Participants will learn how to ride safely and how to legally use critical skills training from MassBike instructors. The event includes a guided ride led by the Wellesley Police Department. During the event, helmets and bikes will be checked for proper fit and safety before moving into brief group instruction and the guided ride. Participants need a rideable bike, closed-toed shoes, and a helmet. Registration is required. (use Activity #231505).

events.title: Call for Walkers: Register for the 38th Annual Jimmy Fund Walk for Dana-Farber presented by Hyundai
events.description: Registration is open for the 2026 Jimmy Fund Walk for Dana-Farber presented by Hyundai. Scheduled for Sunday, Oct. 4, funds raised from the Walk support all forms of adult and pediatric care and cancer research at Dana-Farber Cancer Institute. The 2026 event will aim to raise $10 million in support of Dana-Farber’s mission to defy cancer by leading the way in groundbreaking research and lifesaving patient care. The Details: The Jimmy Fund Walk is the only organized walk permitted to use the famed Boston Marathon® course, and participants have the flexibility to choose from four distance options: 5K walk: Starting at Dana-Farber’s Longwood Medical Campus 10K walk: Starting in Newton Half Marathon walk: Starting in Wellesley Marathon walk: Starting in Hopkinton Along the route, participants will be motivated by Hero posters, photos and quotes from Dana-Farber patients, who are considered the heart and inspiration for the event. Walkers will also enjoy refreshments and cheer squads throughout the course. The event will conclude at the Boston Common Finish Line, by the corner of Charles and Beacon Streets. Finish line activities will include a celebration with food, entertainment, and more. Walkers who prefer to participate virtually can complete their chosen distance from any location. To register for the Jimmy Fund Walk for Dana-Farber or to support a walker, visit JimmyFund.org/Walk or call (866) 531-9255. Registrants can enter the promo code NEWS for $5 off the registration fee. You can also sign up to volunteer on Walk Day.

location.venue: Boston Common by the corner of Charles and Beacon Street
